mechanism_narrative: A downward move in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate applied to equities and eases financial conditions, so 20-day S&P 500 returns should be positive when the policy rate is falling on a 3-month basis.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which should depress near-term earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month after the unemployment rate crosses higher by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time or the signal loses sign consistency in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both improve, the economy is simultaneously showing stronger consumer spending and housing demand, which should lift equity returns over the next quarter because revenue expectations and cyclical sentiment improve together.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-HOUST signal does not outperform either series alone and positive 60-day returns are not more frequent than baseline in the top signal quintile.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rise in INDPRO indicates improving industrial output and supply-side activity, which should support risk assets with a lag as margins and cyclical earnings expectations stabilize over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive monthly changes in INDPRO do not correspond to above-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the rolling 2-year hit rate falls below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is elevated while FEDFUNDS remains below the inflation rate, real policy is accommodative and equities should rally over the next 5 trading days because liquidity conditions are still loose relative to price growth.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by > 1.0 pp
failure_condition: Reject if the spread-based signal fails to produce positive 5-day SPX returns in triggered observations or if the interaction term is not significant after controlling for each series separately.

mechanism_narrative: A stable or falling CPIAUCSL with rising FEDFUNDS implies tightening real rates are less likely to accelerate, so the market should react positively over 20 trading days when inflation is cooling while policy is still restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined disinflation-plus-restrictive-policy pattern does not outperform single-series models or if 20-day returns are negative in the majority of matched cases.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS is rising while UNRATE is falling, labor income and employment breadth are improving together, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through stronger consumption and lower recession risk.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE improvement does not generate higher-than-baseline 20-day returns or if the sign flips in more than one major rolling subperiod.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in GDPC1 implies weakening aggregate demand and lower earnings growth expectations, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next quarter when quarterly real GDP contracts from its prior reading.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP declines are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.02.
